mechanism_narrative: The prevailing federal funds rate level serves as the baseline discount rate for equity valuation. Elevated FEDFUNDS persistently raise the cost of capital and depress risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings forecasts and widen credit spreads, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations with mean return > 0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently elevated readings on retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand strength that fuels corporate revenue surprises and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST are in their top tercile, forward 20-day SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or are negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary policy, elevated CPI readings trigger expectations of further tightening, raising the equity discount rate and compressing valuations, which generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.0%, the Spearman correlation between CPIAUCSL and 60-day forward SPX returns is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production signal weakening goods-sector profitability and involuntary inventory accumulation, leading to negative near-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly changes show a positive and significant correlation (p < 0.05) with subsequent 5-day SPX returns over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment signals rising aggregate income and lower recession probability, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A 3-month increase in PAYEMS fails to coincide with positive average 20-day SPX returns; instead returns are negative in the top PAYEMS-growth tercile.

mechanism_narrative: When aggregate output is above a high threshold, positive GDP surprises amplify overheating concerns and tighten financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 exceeds 11000, subsequent 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of observations over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy and rising unemployment creates a stagflationary drag where elevated discount rates coincide with slowing household income, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In joint high-FEDFUNDS, high-UNRATE regimes, the average 60-day SPX forward return is non-negative or the interaction term in a regression is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
